description: Conversation with errors and user corrections: agent makes mistakes implementing validation

content.text: Add email validation to the User schema. It should check for proper email format and uniqueness.
I'll add email validation to the User schema. Let me update the changeset function.

args.old_content:
def changeset(user, attrs) do
    user
    |> cast(attrs, [:email, :name])
    |> validate_required([:email, :name])
  end
args.new_content:
def changeset(user, attrs) do
    user
    |> cast(attrs, [:email, :name])
    |> validate_required([:email, :name])
    |> validate_format(:email, ~r/@/)
    |> unique_constraint(:email)
  end

content.text: That regex is way too simple. Use a proper email regex like ~r/^[^\s@]+@[^\s@]+\.[^\s@]+$/
You're right, that regex was overly simplistic. Let me fix it with a more robust pattern.
Good. But you forgot to add the index for the unique constraint. The database needs a unique index on the email column.
You're absolutely right - the unique_constraint/2 requires a database unique index to work. Let me create a migration for that.
Now I'll add the unique index to the migration.

args.new_content:
def change do
    create unique_index(:users, [:email])
  end

content.text: Great! Now add a test that verifies duplicate emails are rejected.
I'll add a test for the email uniqueness constraint.

args.new_content:
end

  test "changeset rejects duplicate email" do
    {:ok, _user1} = Accounts.create_user(%{email: "test@example.com", name: "User 1"})
    {:error, changeset} = Accounts.create_user(%{email: "test@example.com", name: "User 2"})
    assert "has already been taken" in errors_on(changeset).email
  end
end

content.text: Looks good! All tests passing now.
